answer:
Honestly, if I had to listen to just one type of music for an entire year, I'd probably go with indie rock. I mean, at first that might seem a little niche, but there's something about the variety within indie rock that keeps it from ever getting boring, you know? It's got energetic tracks, mellow acoustic songs, and even some experimental sounds. For example, bands like Arctic Monkeys or Florence and the Machine have really different vibes—from upbeat tunes to more reflective lyrics—so I’d never feel stuck in one mood.

Also, I find that indie rock kind of resonates with my personality. I'm someone who values authenticity and creativity, and this genre tends to be less commercialized, which makes it feel more genuine. When I'm studying or just relaxing, having something unique and thought-provoking in the background helps me concentrate and feel inspired. Plus, the lyrics often explore emotions, relationships, and even social issues in a way that feels relatable but not too mainstream.

Honestly, another reason is that I’ve discovered so many new bands recently through playlists and recommendations from friends. Exploring those artists gives me a sense of connection, especially now when I'm applying to universities and everything is changing. Having that comfort zone in music feels pretty important. So, while I definitely enjoy other genres—like pop or hip hop—I think indie rock offers enough diversity and depth to keep me engaged for a whole year. Ultimately, I just really appreciate how it combines creativity, emotion, and originality, which matches what I look for in music.
title: If you could listen to only one type of music for a whole year, what would you choose? Explain your reasons for your choice.
